data.title: Royal Bank of Canada Expands Global Stock Presence with New Hire
data.description: The Royal Bank of Canada has appointed a former Goldman Sachs equities trader and execution specialist to spearhead product innovation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's ongoing efforts to enhance its global stock market operations. This strategic move is part of RBC's broader initiative to strengthen its position in the international equities sector.

data.title: STOCKS | High-Priced U.S. Stock Set for Significant Price Reduction
data.description: A prominent stock in the U.S. equity market, known for its high price tag, is poised for a substantial markdown. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the upcoming change that could impact investors and market dynamics. The adjustment is expected to make the stock more accessible to a broader range of investors, potentially increasing its trading volume and liquidity. This move comes amid ongoing discussions about stock pricing strategies and their effects on market participation. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation to assess its implications for the stock's future performance and investor interest.

data.title: Eric Trump Predicts Bitcoin Will Reach $1 Million
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X that Eric Trump expressed his belief that Bitcoin will eventually hit $1 million. Eric Trump, son of Donald Trump, shared his optimistic view on the future value of Bitcoin, suggesting significant growth potential for the cryptocurrency. His statement adds to the ongoing debate about Bitcoin's future valuation and its role in the financial markets.

data.title: Team USA Triumphs Over Sweden in Overtime at Winter Olympics
data.description: Team USA secured a victory against Sweden in a thrilling overtime match during the men's hockey quarterfinals at the Winter Olympics. CNN Breaking News posted on X, highlighting the intense competition that kept fans on the edge of their seats. The game was marked by strong performances from both teams, showcasing their skills and determination. Ultimately, Team USA emerged victorious, advancing to the semifinals and keeping their hopes alive for a medal. This win adds to the excitement and anticipation surrounding the Winter Olympics as teams continue to compete for top honors.

data.title: The World Liberty Forum opened, with WLFI rising 18%. Eric Trump stated that crypto is still at the "starting line."
data.description: On the opening day of the World Liberty Forum held by World Liberty Financial in Mar-a-Lago, its platform token WLFI rose by approximately 18%. Co-founder Eric Trump stated that despite market volatility, the crypto industry as a whole is still in its "one-yard line" stage, meaning it's still in its early stages. Trump pointed out that more and more traditional financial institutions are beginning to offer crypto asset exposure to their clients and are incorporating digital assets into their strategic portfolios, indicating a shift in mainstream capital attitudes. Meanwhile, financial services company Apex Group announced plans to test World Liberty's USD1 stablecoin in its traditional fund management processes, aiming to accelerate the settlement efficiency of tokenized assets. USD1 is currently the fifth-largest USD-pegged stablecoin by market capitalization, with a circulating supply exceeding $5 billion.

data.title: Coinbase expands its on-chain lending collateral assets, adding XRP, DOGE, ADA, and LTC.
data.description: Coinbase announced the addition of XRP, Dogecoin (DOGE), Cardano (ADA), and Litecoin (LTC) as collateral assets to its on-chain crypto-collateralized lending product offered through the DeFi protocol Morpho. Eligible US users (excluding New York State) can now borrow up to $100,000 USDC without selling these tokens. Previously, the product supported Bitcoin and Ethereum as collateral, with a maximum borrowing limit of $5 million USDC for Bitcoin and $1 million USDC for Ethereum. Coinbase stated that the on-chain lending service runs on the Morpho protocol on the Base network, and has disbursed over $1.9 billion in loans to date. The company charges a one-time borrowing fee, which is added to the loan principal.

data.title: OpenAI and Paradigm launch EVMbench to explore the application of AI agents in smart contract security.
data.description: OpenAI and crypto investment firm Paradigm announced a collaboration to launch EVMbench, a tool for evaluating the capabilities of AI agents in smart contract security, including identifying, patching, and exploiting high-risk vulnerabilities. The two companies stated that as AI's capabilities in code writing and execution improve, it can be exploited by attackers or used as a defensive tool. Therefore, it is necessary to test the security capabilities of AI systems in environments with real-world economic significance. EVMbench will offer multiple testing modes, including vulnerability detection, contract modification, exploit elimination, and simulating a complete attack process in a sandbox on-chain environment. This tool's launch comes as DeFi protocol Moonwell and cross-chain liquidity protocol CrossCurve have recently suffered smart contract vulnerability attacks, raising concerns about the security of AI-assisted code. EVMbench is reportedly built on 120 carefully selected vulnerability samples, covering multiple public audits and competition cases. Industry analysts believe that with advancements in AI technology, the threshold for identifying smart contract vulnerabilities may further decrease, and the interplay between security defenses and attack capabilities is accelerating.

data.title: Ether.fi migrates its unmanaged encryption card product from Scroll to OP Mainnet
data.description: Decentralized staking protocol Ether.fi announced that its DeFi-native crypto card product, ether.fi Cash, will be migrated from the Scroll Network to the OP Mainnet (Optimism mainnet). The migration will be completed over the next few months and is expected to involve approximately 70,000 active cards, 300,000 accounts, and millions of dollars in user-locked assets (TVL). ether.fi Cash provides a non-custodial crypto payment experience, allowing users to spend directly with stablecoins or borrow and spend using staked and re-staking assets (such as eETH) as collateral, while continuing to earn yields and enjoy cashback rewards similar to traditional bank cards. The card can be used at all merchants that support Visa. Data shows that Ether.fi cards currently account for nearly half of crypto-native payment card transaction volume, making them one of the leading products in the field. Meanwhile, the OP Stack processed approximately 3.6 billion transactions in the second half of 2025, accounting for 13% of the total crypto transaction volume during the same period, providing high-throughput infrastructure support for this migration.

data.title: Market Experiences Narrow Range Amid Economic Developments
data.description: The market has been trading within a narrow range, reflecting ongoing economic developments. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X, highlighting these trends in their post-market macro note, The Closer. The analysis delves into various factors influencing the market's current state, providing insights into potential future movements. Investors are closely monitoring these developments as they navigate the complexities of the economic landscape. The report emphasizes the importance of understanding market dynamics to make informed investment decisions.

data.title: HPS Leads $700 Million Private-Credit Deal for Elara Caring
data.description: HPS Investment Partners has spearheaded a private-credit transaction valued at approximately $700 million for Elara Caring, a home health-care provider. Bloomberg posted on X, citing an anonymous source familiar with the matter. This deal marks a significant financial move for Elara Caring, which specializes in delivering home-based health care services across the United States. The transaction underscores the growing interest in private credit as a financing option for companies in the health-care sector. Elara Caring's focus on home health care aligns with broader industry trends emphasizing patient-centered care and cost-effective service delivery. The involvement of HPS Investment Partners highlights the firm's active role in facilitating substantial credit deals within the health-care industry. Details regarding the specific terms of the deal or the strategic objectives behind the financing were not disclosed. The transaction reflects ongoing developments in the private credit market, which continues to attract attention from investors seeking alternative investment opportunities. As the health-care industry evolves, financial partnerships like this are expected to play a crucial role in supporting growth and innovation.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC, Signaling Potential Demand Increase
data.description: A recent minting of 250 million USDC stablecoins has been reported by Whale Alert. According to NS3.AI, this event took place at the USDC Treasury, resulting in an increase in the circulating supply. The minting could suggest a rising demand for USDC or a strategic move for liquidity management within the cryptocurrency ecosystem.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Canada and U.S. to Discuss USMCA Review
data.description: Canadian Minister Dominic LeBlanc, responsible for Canada-U.S. trade affairs, announced plans to meet with U.S. Trade Representative Katherine Tai in the coming weeks. According to Jin10, the discussions will focus on reviewing the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA). This meeting aims to address ongoing trade issues and ensure the agreement continues to benefit all parties involved. The USMCA, which replaced the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA), has been a critical framework for trade relations between the three countries.

data.title: Real Estate Tokenization Faces Regulatory Challenges in the U.S.
data.description: Barry Sternlicht, the leader of a $125 billion real estate firm, is exploring the tokenization of real estate assets using blockchain technology. According to NS3.AI, Sternlicht highlights that despite increasing interest in this innovative approach, regulatory obstacles in the United States are hindering advancement. The company plans to provide blockchain-based tokens to its clients once there is clear regulatory guidance.

data.title: Canada and Mexico Align on U.S. Tariff Reduction Efforts
data.description: Canada's Minister for Trade with the United States, Dominic LeBlanc, has highlighted the shared interests between Canada and Mexico in reducing tariffs imposed by the U.S. on certain industries. According to Jin10, LeBlanc emphasized the alignment of both countries in their efforts to address these trade barriers. The collaboration aims to ensure a more balanced trade environment, benefiting industries affected by the tariffs. This development underscores the ongoing trade negotiations and the importance of international cooperation in addressing economic challenges.

data.title: Top S&P 500 Stocks with Over 20% Annualized Returns
data.description: Ten stocks within the S&P 500 have achieved annualized returns exceeding 20% over the past 5, 10, and 20 years. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X. These stocks include NVIDIA Corporation ($NVDA), Lam Research Corporation ($LRCX), KLA Corporation ($KLAC), Amphenol Corporation ($APH), Quanta Services, Inc. ($PWR), Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. ($MPWR), Comfort Systems USA, Inc. ($FIX), EMCOR Group, Inc. ($EME), Axon Enterprise, Inc. ($AXON), and Texas Pacific Land Corporation ($TPL). These companies have demonstrated consistent growth and performance, making them notable within the S&P 500 index.

data.title: UK Government Proposes New Rules for Tech Companies on Abusive Content
data.description: The UK government has introduced a proposal mandating tech companies to eliminate abusive images from their platforms within 48 hours. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the urgency of addressing harmful content online. This initiative is part of a broader effort to enhance online safety and protect users from harmful material. The proposed regulations aim to hold tech firms accountable for the swift removal of such content, ensuring a safer digital environment for all users. The government emphasizes the importance of these measures in safeguarding individuals from online abuse and exploitation.

data.title: European Commission President to Visit Greenland in March
data.description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen is scheduled to visit Greenland in March. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this visit in strengthening ties between the European Union and Greenland. The visit is expected to focus on discussions around economic cooperation and environmental issues. This trip underscores the EU's interest in the Arctic region, particularly in light of its strategic and environmental importance. Further details on the agenda and specific meetings are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: Hedera Faces Bearish Pressure Amid Potential Liquidation Risk
data.description: Hedera (HBAR) is experiencing bearish pressure with a notable number of short futures contracts open, posing a risk of $4.9 million in liquidations if the price exceeds $0.1143. According to NS3.AI, the Chaikin Money Flow indicator is showing initial signs of capital inflows, suggesting a potential short-term recovery. Additionally, HBAR's correlation with Bitcoin has decreased, which may allow it to diverge from broader market trends. Key technical levels to watch include resistance at $0.1035 and support at $0.0961; failure to maintain support could result in further declines.

data.title: CME Predicts Low Probability of Fed Rate Cuts by March
data.description: The CME's FedWatch tool indicates a mere 5.9% chance of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by 25 basis points by March, with a 94.1% likelihood of rates remaining unchanged. According to Jin10, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut by April stands at 20.5%, while the chance of maintaining current rates is 78.5%. Additionally, there is a 1.0% probability of a cumulative 50 basis point cut by April. By June, the likelihood of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut increases to 49.8%.
